name: Alabama SNAP (Food Assistance Program)
description: Alabama's food assistance program providing monthly EBT benefits to purchase groceries for eligible low-income households. Administered by the Alabama Department of Human Resources.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 130% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: Alabama Medicaid
description: Alabama's Medicaid program providing health coverage for eligible low-income residents including doctor visits, hospital stays, prescriptions, and long-term care services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income children, pregnant women, elderly, and individuals with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Children up to 317% FPL; pregnant women up to 146% FPL; aged/disabled at SSI level

name: Alabama Unemployment Compensation
description: Provides temporary wage replacement benefits to Alabama workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Administered by the Alabama Department of Labor.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage/work requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: Alabama Housing Finance Authority (AHFA)
description: Provides affordable housing resources including down payment assistance, rental programs, and tax credit developments for Alabama residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally targets households at or below 80% AMI

name: Legal Services Alabama
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Alabamians in matters including housing, family law, public benefits, consumer issues, and disaster recovery.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals at or below 125% of the federal poverty level

name: Alabama Career Center System
description: Alabama's workforce development network providing job search assistance, training programs, resume help, and career counseling through local career centers statewide.
eligibilitySummary: All Alabama residents seeking employment or career development services

name: Alabama LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income households pay heating and cooling bills. Administered through community action agencies across Alabama.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households with home energy costs, prioritizing elderly, disabled, and young children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% FPL

name: Alabama 211
description: Free, confidential referral service connecting Alabamians with local health and human services resources including food, housing, utilities, and crisis support.
eligibilitySummary: All Alabama residents seeking assistance or referrals
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter

name: Community Food Bank of Central Alabama
description: Feeding America affiliate serving 35 counties in central Alabama. Distributes food through partner agencies, food pantries, and feeding programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in the central Alabama service area

name: Food Bank of North Alabama
description: Feeding America affiliate serving 11 counties in northern Alabama. Provides food distribution and hunger relief programs to combat food insecurity.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in the northern Alabama service area

name: Feeding the Gulf Coast
description: Feeding America affiliate serving southern Alabama and the Gulf Coast region. Provides food distribution through a network of partner agencies and programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in the Gulf Coast region

name: Alabama Department of Veterans Affairs
description: Assists Alabama veterans and their families in obtaining federal and state benefits including healthcare, disability compensation, education, and burial benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Veterans, active duty service members, and their dependents residing in Alabama

name: Crisis Services of North Alabama
description: Provides crisis intervention services including a 24-hour crisis line, mobile crisis teams, and crisis stabilization for mental health and substance abuse emergencies.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in northern Alabama experiencing a mental health or substance abuse crisis

name: Alabama Child Care Subsidy Program
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income families pay for childcare so parents can work, attend school, or participate in training programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families with children under age 13 who need childcare
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 85% SMI

name: Alabama TANF (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families)
description: Cash assistance program for low-income families with children, providing monthly benefits and supportive services to help families achieve self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with dependent children who meet work requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income thresholds; varies by family size

name: Alabama WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children supplemental nutrition program providing healthy foods, nutrition education, and breastfeeding support for eligible participants.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children up to age 5

name: Alabama Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides energy efficiency improvements to low-income homes to reduce energy costs. Includes insulation, weather-stripping, and heating/cooling system improvements.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters, with priority for elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% FPL

name: Birmingham-Jefferson County Transit Authority (BJCTA)
description: Public transit system serving the Birmingham metropolitan area with fixed-route bus service and paratransit for the greater Birmingham region.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders in the Birmingham metro area

name: Alabama Head Start Association
description: Federally funded early childhood education program promoting school readiness for children from birth to age 5 in low-income Alabama families.
eligibilitySummary: Families with children birth to age 5 at or below federal poverty guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: 100% FPL or below; foster and homeless children automatically eligible

name: Alabama Department of Rehabilitation Services
description: Provides vocational rehabilitation, independent living, and early intervention services for Alabamians with disabilities to help them achieve employment and independence.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Alabama Housing Authority - Section 8
description: Administers the Housing Choice Voucher program providing rental assistance to low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals across Alabama.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 50% AMI for initial eligibility

name: United Way of Central Alabama
description: Provides community programs and funding for education, financial stability, and health services in the Birmingham and central Alabama area.
eligibilitySummary: Residents of the central Alabama region

name: Alabama Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, shelter referrals, and support for victims of domestic violence operated by the Alabama Coalition Against Domestic Violence.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or affected by domestic violence in Alabama

name: Alabama Arise
description: Statewide nonprofit advocacy organization working on poverty-related policy issues including healthcare access, tax reform, and public benefits in Alabama.
eligibilitySummary: All Alabama residents; focuses on advocacy for low-income Alabamians

name: Alabama Possible (formerly Alabama Possible)
description: Provides economic empowerment through programs including tax preparation assistance (VITA), financial coaching, and advocacy for policies that address poverty.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families in Alabama

name: Huntsville Housing Authority
description: Provides affordable public housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) for low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in Huntsville.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the Huntsville area
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; generally at or below 80% AMI

name: Alabama ALL Kids (CHIP)
description: Alabama's Children's Health Insurance Program providing affordable health coverage for uninsured children in families earning too much for Medicaid but unable to afford private insurance.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 in families with income above Medicaid limits but below CHIP thresholds
incomeLimitNotes: Children in families up to 317% FPL

name: Mobile County Community Action Agency
description: Provides comprehensive anti-poverty services in Mobile County including emergency assistance, energy assistance, housing, and workforce development.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in Mobile County

name: Alabama Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounts on monthly telephone or broadband service for qualifying low-income Alabama households to maintain access to essential communications.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: 135% FPL or participation in qualifying federal assistance program

name: Alabama Adult Education
description: Provides free GED preparation, adult basic education, English as a Second Language, and workforce literacy programs at locations across the state.
eligibilitySummary: Adults 16 and older who are not enrolled in school and need basic education or GED preparation

name: Montgomery Area Transit System (MATS)
description: Public transit system serving the Montgomery metropolitan area with fixed-route bus service and paratransit options.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders in the Montgomery metro area
